message:
With the summer heat rolling in, here's something worth knowing about how your dog actually stays cool: they barely sweat the way we do. 🐶☀️

Almost all of a dog's sweat glands are tucked into the pads of their paws — so on a hot day you might spot little damp paw prints across the kitchen floor. But that tiny bit of sweat isn't what cools them down. Their real air conditioning is panting: every fast breath evaporates moisture from the tongue and mouth and carries the heat away.

That's also why a thick-coated dog can overheat surprisingly fast — they can't just sweat it out like we can. On hot days, shade, fresh water, and skipping the midday walk go a long way. 💛

Comment 🐾 if your pup turns into a melted puddle the second it gets warm!
